content.problemStatement:
There is a fair going on in Chefland. Chef wants to visit the fair along with his NNN friends. Chef manages to collect KKK passes for the fair. Will Chef be able to enter the fair with all his NNN friends?

A person can enter the fair using one pass, and each pass can be used by only one person.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing two space-separated integers N,KN, KN,K.


Output Format
For each test case, print on a new line `YES` if Chef will be able to enter the fair with all his NNN friends and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in either uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `yEs`, `yes`, `Yes`, and `YES` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and K, where N is the number of Chef's friends and K is the number of passes collected.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if Chef and all his friends can enter the fair (i.e., K ≥ N+1), otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: total needed = 5+1=6 ≤ 8 → YES.
Test case 2: total needed = 6+1=7 > 3 → NO.
Test case 3: total needed = 2+1=3 > 2 → NO.
Test case 4: total needed = 1+1=2 ≤ 2 → YES.
Case 1: need 2 passes but have 1 → NO.\nCase 2: need 101 passes and have 101 → YES.
Case 1: need 101 passes, only 100 available → NO.\nCase 2: need 100 passes, have 100 → YES.

solution.approach: For each test case compute the total number of people that need a pass: Chef plus his N friends, i.e., total = N + 1. Compare this total with the available passes K. If K is greater than or equal to total, print YES; otherwise print NO. This is O(1) work per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
